preamble: The phonetic transformation from the diphthong /au/ to /oi/ is an attested sound change, observed in the historical development of certain language families. This shift typically involves a change in the articulation of both elements of the diphthong, leading to a more fronted and unrounded second element.
phoneticEffects: This shift primarily involves the fronting and unrounding of the second vocalic element of the diphthong, moving from a high back rounded vowel ([u]) to a high front unrounded vowel ([i])

examples.note: Latin *au evolved to /oi/ in Fala, as seen in the word for 'gold'.
